title: rubus hirtus var. gutinensis
intro: Rubus hirtus var. gutinensis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a type of bramble plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. gutinensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and infusion, making it a versatile remedy for various ailments.
